content: The synthetically produced radioactive noble gas by the collision of ${ }_{98}^{249} \mathrm{Cf}$ with ${ }_{20}^{48} \mathrm{Ca}$ is
explanation:
The reaction of ${ }_{98}^{249} \mathrm{Cf}$ and ${ }_{20}^{40} \mathrm{Ca}$ takes place as follows

$${ }_{98}^{249} \mathrm{Cf}+{ }_{20}^{40} \mathrm{Ca} \longrightarrow{ }_{118}^{294} \mathrm{Og}+3{ }_0^1 n$$

Thus, the radioactive noble gas formed is Oganesson $(Z=118)$
